mods.name: IFZ Mod API
mods.summary: Shared library other IFZMods depend on. Controller cache, time/night helpers, VFX + reflection utilities. Install if a mod lists it as required.

sections.description:
Shared helper library for Infection Free Zone BepInEx mods. Loads first (filename 000_IFZModAPI.dll). Provides a cached game-controller registry (Buildings / Groups / Squads / Stockrooms / Work / ColorSwitcher / Weather / Light), time + night-blend helpers, VFX helpers (real smoke prefab clone, pooled point lights), cached reflection, and a PostProcessing FloatParameter shim.

You only need this if another mod lists it as a requirement. It does nothing on its own.
sections.installation: Drop 000_IFZModAPI.dll into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Nothing to configure. Required by ArmyBackup, Cinematic FX, and Smart Worker Redistribution (and possibly others over time).
sections.mainFeatures:
- Loads before all other IFZMods (alphabetical + BepInDependency)
- Shared controller-instance cache
- Time / sunrise / sunset / NightBlend helpers
- VFX: real Smoke prefab clone, pooled point-light flashes
- Cached reflection + FloatParameter shim

mods.fileNotes: 1.4.4: Harmony __args compatibility fix — removes dependency on newer HarmonyX __args injection (which old BepInEx 5.4.5.0 can't emit), so patches apply cleanly on old BepInEx. Fixes GiveOrder patch failure on Chinese-repo old versions (SquadMerge merge hook never ran). Public-surface unchanged; backward-compat audited. 1.4.3: fixes the clock showing day '0' (e.g. '0 JULY') after loading a base-game save - the day-counter restore fired the clock-repaint event redundantly and the UI painted the wrong number; now it only fires when the value actually changes. Gameplay day was always correct. Builds on 1.4.2's expedition day-counter fix + Time.RunDay. IMPORTANT: update ALL your IFZ mods together - older versions crash with this API.

mods.name: Army Backup AI Fix
mods.summary: Request Backup Army tanks actually engage hostiles near your base instead of idling.
mods.description:
Fixes the broken "Request Backup" AI. Vanilla Army tanks spawn and sit. With this mod they sweep player buildings on a configurable tick, find the nearest Infected or Bandit threat inside BaseDefenceRadius, and issue an AttackGroupOrder via the game's own OrdersHandlers. Includes hysteresis (ReassignDistance) so they don't dither between targets, separate toggles for Infected vs Bandits, configurable engagement range, and verbose logging.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled toggle in `[General]`. Tickrate, radii, target faction filter, hysteresis distance all live-tunable.

sections.description: Fixes the broken "Request Backup" AI. Vanilla Army backup tanks spawn and sit. With this mod, every 4 seconds (configurable) the mod walks all Army groups, finds the nearest Infected or Bandit threat inside the player base radius, and issues an AttackGroupOrder via the game's own OrdersHandlers.

mods.name: Cinematic FX
mods.summary: Buffed blood and tracers, demolish dust, impact craters, night thunderstorms.
mods.description:
Visual polish pass. Five independent subsystems, each toggleable:

- **Burning Structures** — buildings below configurable HP threshold emit cloned in-game smoke from their roof.
- **Buffed Blood** — scales blood splash particle size and emission rate.
- **Buffed Tracers** — widens bullet tracer LineRenderer.
- **Demolish Dust** — dust burst on deconstruct completion.
- **Impact Craters** — extra smoke puff on explosion sites.
- **Night Storms** — thunder rolls during night after configurable game day.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`, per-feature toggles under each subsystem section.

mods.name: Darker Nights
mods.summary: Actually-dark nights, full-moon brightness bonus, smooth dawn/dusk, day tone-shaping.
mods.description:
Owns the day/night lighting curve. Patches the game's ColorSwitcher source fields (`saturation_Day_PP`, `contrast_Day_PP`, `blue_Day_PP`) so the per-tick lerp uses our values as the basis — outputs don't get clobbered by the game.

- **Night** — configurable sun-intensity factor (0 = pitch black, 1 = vanilla), absolute ambient color + intensity, AutoExposure clamp. Full-moon nights apply a brightness multiplier on top.
- **Day** — saturation / contrast / warmth / post-exposure boost so daylight doesn't feel flat. Tunable per slider.
- **Smooth transitions** — configurable TransitionHours fade window around sunrise / sunset. Pair with GunfireLights' matching value for synchronized tower-light fade-in.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

mods.name: Gunfire Lights
mods.summary: Real-time point lights for muzzle flashes, explosions, headlights, tower searchlights, antenna beacons.
mods.description:
Adds dynamic lighting to combat and infrastructure:

- **Muzzle flashes** — pooled point light per gun, weapon-specific color tint (heavy / shotgun warmer).
- **Explosions** — orange flash with configurable range and intensity.
- **Vehicle headlights** — forward-facing spot light on moving vehicles at night.
- **Tower searchlights** — slow-sweeping spot light on defensive towers, holds on last-attacked target.
- **Antenna aviation beacons** — real-world FAA-style red/green blinking lights on antenna towers, 24/7 or night-only.

All lights fade in/out across a configurable TransitionHours window around sunrise / sunset. Pair with DarkerNights' matching value for synchronized lighting.

Light pool of 32 reused across all flashes. No shadow casting (cheap).

**Defaults are now the cinematic showcase look** — punchy muzzle flashes (`[Muzzle] Intensity = 115`), fat glow (`[Glow] MuzzleSize = 1.1` / `ExplosionSize = 4.5`), and a real searchlight per gun barrel (`[Performance] OneLightPerTower = false`). **For more FPS** with many towers, set `OneLightPerTower = true` (one searchlight per tower instead of per barrel) — the heaviest setting; dial it back first if your FPS drops.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`.

mods.name: House Power
mods.summary: Powered homes get a +mood bonus, chimneys emit smoke, entrance lights at night.
mods.description:
Residential buildings auto-install a power generator that consumes wood on a configurable tick. While powered:

- **+mood bonus** — separate row in the mood panel, scales with the fraction of residents in powered homes. Live UI update.
- **Chimney smoke** — clones the in-game worksite smoke at chimney prop, falls back to roof centre if no chimney prop. High-rises without chimneys skipped.
- **Entrance / window lights** — small warm point lights at door and window props, brighter at night, dimmer at day. Throttled to 2 Hz to keep cost down across hundreds of buildings.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`. Wood-per-tick, tick interval, mood bonus magnitude, light range and intensity all tunable.

mods.name: House Rebalance
mods.summary: Citizens auto-migrate to higher-priority housing when capacity exists.
mods.description:
Vanilla citizens stay assigned to whatever house they got first. With this mod, every TickSeconds (default 30) each citizen re-evaluates housing via the game's own `FindBestHouse` ranking — they walk to a better house if one has slots. No more "want better housing" mood penalty while empty mansions sit unused.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`. Tick interval tunable.

mods.summary: Bundle of small QoL tweaks: TowerHMG, VehicleAmmoFix, BuildingIconToggle, ShellLairs. Each toggleable in F1. (Height and clay-pit features moved to the new HighGround and ClayPitFixes mods.)
mods.fileNotes: 1.5.2 BETA - Mortar squads/bunkers shell infected lairs + occupied buildings via right-click (real ammo + retreat); exits vehicle/building first. HMG vehicle ammo-drain fixed. Caveats: few rounds post-clear; exit vehicle first; mind low-ammo squads.
mods.description:
Several small quality-of-life tweaks bundled in one DLL. Each feature is individually toggleable in F1 ConfigurationManager.

NOTE: as of 1.5.1 the height/elevation features (TallBuildings, ElevatedFiring, HeightAdvantage) moved to the new HighGround mod, and clay-pit regen/demolish moved to the new ClayPitFixes mod. Install those separately if you want them.

Main features:
- TowerHMG: defensive towers accept Heavy MGs (eq_hcal) once carworkshop_hcal is researched.
- VehicleAmmoFix: vehicles auto-refill ammo from their own trunk cargo when the stockroom path fails.
- BuildingIconToggle: hotkey (default I) to hide/show all building icons; state persists.
- ShellLairs: vanilla won't let you attack-order infected lairs. With mortar squad(s) selected, right-click a revealed infected lair to shell it; plain infantry still enter to clear as normal. Selected bunkers/towers that have a mortar weapon also shell the lair on the same right-click.

mods.name: Locale Fix (ConfigurationManager floats)
mods.summary: Forces InvariantCulture so ConfigurationManager can edit decimal floats on comma-locale systems.
mods.description:
Required if you're on a comma-decimal Windows locale (nl-NL, de-DE, fr-FR, etc.) and you've seen `FormatException: Input string was not in a correct format` when editing float fields in the F1 ConfigurationManager UI.

Forces `CultureInfo.InvariantCulture` on the main thread and `DefaultThreadCurrentCulture`. A Sentinel MonoBehaviour re-pins every frame in case Unity / game subsystems reset it on scene loads.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`. Note: the Awake-time pin applies once at process start regardless of toggle. Off-during-session stops the per-frame Sentinel re-pin but doesn't undo the initial pin.

mods.name: Performance Pack
mods.summary: Late-game FPS fixes. Headline: hideout patrol spawn-point LOD ~doubles night FPS on old saves — same horde, no gameplay change.
mods.fileNotes: 1.5.2 - HUD now shows active lair count and a low-fps 'why' cause line (sticky). Reads existing engine counters only; no cost when the HUD is hidden.
mods.description:
A bundle of safe, measured late-game performance patches. Headline fix (1.4.0):

- **Hideout patrol spawn-point LOD** — old/large maps accumulate tens of thousands of dormant patrol spawn points (the game's lightweight 'virtual' form of a patrol). The base game updates EVERY one every frame (path-move + terrain raycast + fog) and leaves a debug-only physics collider live on each — at ~19k points that was the single biggest night-time cost (measured ~50ms/frame of it). PerfPack distance-LODs them: off-screen points update less often with time-compensated movement (roaming/despawn stay accurate), near-camera points are untouched, and the inert debug colliders are disabled. **Horde size is unchanged** — same number of patrols, they just stop burning the CPU while off-screen. Roughly **doubled night FPS** on a deep save (12-13 -> 26-28) with no perceptible gameplay change.

Also included:

- **A\* graph-update throttle** — spreads navmesh rebuilds (build/demolish/wall-damage) across frames instead of one fat main-thread stall.
- **Enemy 'nearest building' AI cache** — the raid/siege AI re-scans a group's entire in-range building set every behaviour tick; cached per group for a short TTL.
- **Billboard throttle** — refreshes each `Billboard.Update` every Nth frame, staggered.
- **Blood-decay throttle** — clamps `SetBloodPuddleValue` writes to once every IntervalSec per body.
- **PerfScheduler** — frame-budget drain queue + stagger foundation.
- **Opt-in diagnostics** (off by default) — frame-spike profiler + per-method CPU sampler for diagnosing hitches.

All patches are config-gated and reversible. Safe defaults — no rendering or logic regressions.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master Enabled in `[General]`, per-feature toggles under each section.

mods.name: Unlocked Buildings
mods.summary: Bypass "too narrow / too complex" on splits, lift the custom-building restrictions (size + wall-length), and uncap the per-building storage / population / deconstruct-yield ceiling.
mods.description:
Lets you cut up castles, cathedrals, and other irregular generated shapes that the vanilla split validator rejects — and lifts the restrictions on the draw-your-own custom buildings.

**Splits.** The game's `BuildingSplitHandler.CheckWrongDimensions` enforces three thresholds via `SplitConfig`:
- polygon surface ≤ MinSplitSurface × 3 → SmallSurface
- offset-polygon shrink by WrongDimensionDelta produces ≠1 sub-poly → WrongDimensions
- any source node further than MaxDistanceFromOffsetPolygon from the shrunk poly → WrongDimensions

This mod flips a denied result to None when you opt in. Two independent toggles: `AllowWrongDimensions` (the narrow/complex check) and `AllowSmallSurface` (the size check), both default on.

**Custom buildings (`[BuildingSize]`).** Also lifts the restrictions on the draw-your-own buildings:
- `OverrideBuildingSize` + `MaxBuildingSurface` / `MinBuildingSurface` — vanilla caps custom buildings at 1000 m² (min 5); raise the max to draw bigger. Defaults match vanilla, so the size change is opt-in. Construction cost scales with area, so big footprints get expensive.
- `AllowShortWalls` — bypasses the "at least one of the walls is too short" rejection (vanilla `MinWallLength`), so you can draw tight/irregular footprints.

**Caveat:** splits or builds along genuinely degenerate geometry may produce ugly or partially-walkable parts. The mod doesn't make them better — it lets them happen.

**Per-building capacity (`[BuildingCapacity]`, opt-in).** Vanilla clamps every building's effective volume to `AdaptConfig.BuildingsMaxVolume` (~50000 m³), and that single clamped volume drives storage capacity, population / gathering-place capacity, max HP, and deconstruction yield. The result: a 90 m highrise hits the same ~2000-per-building wall as a 30 m block (splitting works around it because each piece gets its own volume). Turn on `UncapBuildingCapacity` and set `VolumeMultiplier` (default 3) to lift that ceiling so bigger buildings actually hold, house, and yield more — no splitting required. WARNING: population scales too, so very high multipliers mean far more residents/workers and a late-game FPS cost; 2-3 is a safe start. HP and full-adapt construction cost scale as well, so giant buildings get tougher and pricier.

**Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.** Master split toggle is `[General] Enabled`; the building-size override is independent in `[BuildingSize]`; the capacity uncap is independent in `[BuildingCapacity]` (off by default). Pick only the features you want.

mods.name: Squad Grenades
mods.summary: Idle squads automatically throw grenades and fire mortars at hostile clusters already in range - including from inside buildings and moving vehicles. Friendly-fire safe.
mods.description:
**Stop micro-managing grenades.** Idle squads that carry grenades automatically lob them at clusters of infected or raiders that are *already within throwing range* - they never advance toward the horde, they just use what is in reach.

Vanilla never auto-throws grenades (you have to hand-aim every one), so this is pure quality-of-life: squads holding the line, garrisoned in a building, or rolling in a vehicle will pitch grenades at the right moment on their own. Mortar teams auto-fire too.

**Friendly-fire safe:** a throw is cancelled if the blast would catch your own squad, another player squad, or any friendly - rescued survivors, civilians, vendors, allied army. Hostiles only.

**Works everywhere the squad does:**
- From inside buildings
- From a moving vehicle - the squad keeps driving while it throws (passengers lob, the driver drives)
- On expeditions, where auto-grenades really shine against the loot-zone hordes

**Tunable in F1 (ConfigurationManager):** master toggle, grenades vs mortars, blast radius, minimum cluster size before it bothers, per-squad cooldown, and the self/friendly safety margins.

mods.name: Swarm Fix
mods.summary: Restores late-game infected night waves that silently stop. The swarm aggro overflows past the top tier (Infinity/NaN) so no wave ever spawns again while raiders keep attacking; this sanitises the tier lookup so the hordes come back.
mods.fileNotes: 1.1.0 - first release. Fixes late-game infected swarms that stop spawning once swarm aggro overflows past the top tier (to Infinity, or NaN after a reload). Healthy saves unaffected. Corrupted-save horde size tunable in F1 (GarbageHordeScale).

sections.description:
On long Infection Free Zone saves the nightly infected swarms quietly stop coming - while raiders keep attacking (the tell). Cause: the game's swarm 'aggro' value compounds every wave and eventually overflows past its largest swarm tier all the way to +Infinity; the save can't store Infinity, so after a reload it comes back as NaN. The wave picker (SwarmConfig.GetSwarm) only matches a tier where Min <= aggro <= Max, so once aggro is Infinity / NaN / past the top tier, nothing matches and no swarm is ever spawned again.

SwarmFix adds a Harmony prefix that sanitises that lookup so a tier always resolves: healthy saves are untouched (normal scaling), legitimately high aggro clamps to the top tier, and corrupted aggro falls back to a tunable fraction of the top tier (GarbageHordeScale, default = max horde). It only touches the lookup - your saved aggro is never modified, so it's non-destructive and fully reversible.

Configurable in F1 ConfigurationManager.
sections.installation:
Drop the DLL into <game>/BepInEx/plugins/. Launch the game. Press F1 in-game (ConfigurationManager) to tweak settings live.

Proton/Wine/Mac: add launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b" %command%.

mods.name: Night Lights
mods.summary: Warm amber window lights across the city at night. Reconstructs the game's own window placement and lights a realistic, patchy fraction of windows after dark.
mods.description:
Brings the city to life after dark. At dusk, windows across occupied buildings warm up with amber light — a patchy, lived-in scatter rather than every pane blazing, so it reads like a real city at night.

**How it works:** Night Lights reconstructs the game's *own* window layout (same UV mapping, same per-building window texture and slice) and draws a second, additive emissive pass on top — so the glow lands exactly on the painted windows and is immune to the moonlight wash that locks the native windows blue-white at night. It fades in/out around sunrise/sunset using the game's own day/night value.

**Tunable in F1 (ConfigurationManager):** colour, intensity/bloom, the lit fraction (how many windows light up), night ramp timing, and a set of cleanup knobs. Master toggle in `[General] Enabled`.

**Limitations (please read):**
- Placement is pixel-accurate on standard apartment/house buildings. The game decides *which* windows exist per building through internal shader logic this mod does not fully replicate, so on some buildings a few extra cells can light up near roof edges or building corners where the game itself left brick. The patchy `[Lit] LitFraction` default is tuned so these read as just another occupied flat rather than an obvious error — but if you look closely at edges you may spot them. Cleanup knobs (`AlphaCutoff`, `RoofGate`, `BorderKill`) let you trim them further.
- Lights only the game's standard adapted buildings; some special/decorative structures won't glow.

**Performance:** Light. The mod adds one extra additive draw pass per adapted building (the window emission), with a simple fragment shader, no shadows, no extra real-time lights. CPU cost is a periodic re-scan of buildings (default every 1s) plus the extra draw calls; GPU cost is minor additive overdraw. On very large late-game cities the extra draw calls add some render-thread cost like any per-building visual mod — toggle it off in F1 if you're FPS-bound. The shader bundle is embedded in the DLL, so there's nothing extra to install.

mods.name: Buildable Bridges
mods.summary: Drag-build a walkable bridge across water with the wall tool. Real road, deck and railings; units and vehicles cross; survives save/load.
mods.description:
Build real bridges over water.

Press **B** in-game to enter bridge mode, then drag from one bank to the other exactly like placing a wall. The span builds as a proper bridge — road surface, deck and railings deformed per span — and becomes a walkable crossing: your units *and* vehicles route across it. Bridges cost wall materials by length and persist across save/load.

**How it works:** it reuses the game's own wall placement (so drag, snap, cost and saving all come from vanilla) and the game's native bridge builder to lay real road/deck/railing geometry at bank height, then stamps a walkable road surface so pathfinding connects the two shores.

**Tunable in F1 (ConfigurationManager):** hotkey, deck width, bank-overhang, height offset, and a debug routing-strip overlay (off by default).

mods.name: NoPath - YesPath
mods.summary: Demolish the 'no path' buildings along the water that you normally can't get rid of. A real worker walks in and tears them down under normal rules — no instant teardown.
mods.description:
Buildings placed along the water often end up stuck with a red **"no path"** — workers can't route to them, so you can never demolish them and they sit on your map forever.

NoPath - YesPath fixes that. When you order a demolish on one of these buildings, the mod opens the building's own footprint to pathfinding and **injects a reachable work spot onto it**, so a real worker walks *into* the footprint and demolishes it from the inside — under full vanilla rules (real worker, normal teardown time). No instant removal, no cheats: it just makes the building reachable so the normal demolish can happen.

**What it can and can't fix:**
- **Buildings on a shore ledge** (the building sits on land, only its work spots spilled onto the water) — **fixed**. A worker now reaches it and demolishes it.
- **Buildings sitting literally on the water** (no land under them at all) — these are true isolates. The engine can't put a walkable surface on water, so no worker can ever reach them under vanilla rules. The mod logs these so you know which is which, but it won't (and by design doesn't) force-remove them.

**Tunable in F1 (ConfigurationManager):** master toggle, and whether a cancelled demolish re-blocks the footprint.

Zero Harmony patches (Zenject signal + reflection), Wine/Proton-safe.

Thanks to **Meefstick (GZW)** for the report, the "demolish from the inside" idea, and the in-game testing.
